DSDS 0.1
Overview Root Schema
example extensions link metadata presentation purpose rich-text status
component pattern style theme token
accessibility anatomy api best-practice content design-specifications guideline interaction motion principle scale state variant

DSDS Presentation Definitions

Presentation types used across DSDS for visual and interactive demonstrations. Each presentation is an independent typed object representing a specific media format: images, videos, code snippets, or URLs. These definitions are referenced by the example schema and any other schema that needs to embed a visual or interactive presentation.

Source: common/presentation.schema.json

4 definitions in this file:

  • presentationImage
  • presentationVideo
  • presentationCode
  • presentationUrl

presentationImage

An image presentation — a screenshot, diagram, annotated mockup, or exported design frame.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
kind"image"requiredIdentifies this presentation as an image.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL or relative path to the image file.
altstringrequiredAlternative text describing the content of the image. MUST be provided for accessibility.

presentationVideo

A video presentation — a screen recording, animated transition, or interaction walkthrough.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
kind"video"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a video.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL or relative path to the video file.
altstringrequiredAlternative text describing the content of the video. MUST be provided for accessibility.

presentationCode

A code presentation — a source code snippet showing how to use the artifact.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
kind"code"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a code snippet.
codestringrequiredThe source code of the example.
languagestringrequiredThe programming language or syntax (e.g., 'jsx', 'tsx', 'html', 'css', 'vue', 'svelte', 'swift', 'kotlin').

presentationUrl

A URL presentation — a link to any web resource such as a live demo, interactive example, documentation page, or hosted component story. Use for Storybook stories, CodeSandbox embeds, StackBlitz projects, or any other addressable web content.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
kind"url"requiredIdentifies this presentation as a URL reference to a web resource.
urlstring (uri)requiredURL to the web resource (e.g., 'https://storybook.acme.com/?path=/story/components-button--primary', 'https://codesandbox.io/s/button-example').
